title: Incident #001 — Conference Check-in Terminal

briefing.context: You're helping build a self-service check-in kiosk for a technology conference.
briefing.incidentReport: The current kiosk only prints raw data, making badges unreadable for attendees.
briefing.task: Write a function generate_badge(name, age, city) that returns a neatly formatted attendee badge.
constraints: Do not print directly; return the completed badge string.
Preserve the name exactly as entered.
Age should appear as a number in the output.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #001 — Conference Check-in Terminal

The conference badge printer currently outputs messy information. Create generate_badge(name, age, city) that returns a professional attendee badge.

hints.text: You need one final string that combines all three pieces of information.
Python has a convenient way to insert variables directly into strings.
The function should return the completed string rather than printing it.

disallow.reason: Return the badge instead of printing it.

onFailFeedback.generic: The returned badge doesn't match the requested format. Check spacing and punctuation.

successMessage: Badge printer restored. Attendees now receive readable conference badges.

title: Incident #002 — Coffee Shop Receipt Generator

briefing.context: A local café needs an automatic receipt generator.
briefing.incidentReport: Cashiers are calculating totals manually, causing frequent mistakes.
briefing.task: Write create_receipt(item, quantity, price) that returns a formatted receipt including the total cost.
constraints: Total = quantity × price.
Return the receipt as a multi-line string.
Display the total with two decimal places.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #002 — Coffee Shop Receipt Generator

Create create_receipt(item, quantity, price) that returns a clean receipt.

hints.text: You'll need to calculate the total before building the output.
Format decimal values using :.2f inside an f-string.
Return one string containing multiple lines separated by \n.

testCases.expected:
Item: Latte
Quantity: 2
Unit Price: $4.50
Total: $9.00

Item: Tea
Quantity: 1
Unit Price: $2.00
Total: $2.00

Item: Cookie
Quantity: 5
Unit Price: $1.20
Total: $6.00

disallow.reason: Return the receipt rather than printing it.

onFailFeedback.generic: Verify the arithmetic and formatting, especially the currency precision.

title: Incident #018 — Password Security Audit

briefing.context: You're part of the cybersecurity team at a software company.
briefing.incidentReport: Thousands of weak passwords are making it through the registration system because no validation is being performed.
briefing.task: Write validate_password(password) that returns a report describing whether the password contains an uppercase letter, lowercase letter, digit, special character, its length, and whether it is considered strong.
constraints: A strong password must be at least 8 characters long.
It must contain an uppercase letter, lowercase letter, digit, and special character.
Return a dictionary describing the results.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #018 — Password Security Audit

Implement validate_password(password) that analyses a password and returns a validation report.

hints.text: Inspect each character individually.
String methods like isupper(), islower(), and isdigit() may be useful.
Build up boolean values while looping through the password.

disallow.reason: Return the report instead of printing it.

onFailFeedback.generic: Check how you're identifying character types and computing the final strength.

successMessage: Security audit passed. Weak passwords are now detected.

title: Incident #027 — Faulty Temperature Sensor

briefing.context: A weather station stores every sensor reading as raw text.
briefing.incidentReport: Engineers can't process the readings until they're converted into usable numeric values.
briefing.task: Write process_reading(reading) that converts the string into a numeric value and returns useful statistics.
constraints: The input is always a valid decimal string.
Return the original float, integer truncation, rounded value (1 decimal place), and detected Python type.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #027 — Faulty Temperature Sensor

Implement process_reading(reading) to convert and summarize a raw sensor reading.

hints.text: The reading arrives as text.
Convert it before performing mathematical operations.
type() can help report the detected data type.

onFailFeedback.generic: Verify your numeric conversions and rounding logic.

title: Incident #051 — Smart Parking Garage

briefing.context: You're writing the access controller for a multi-level smart parking garage.
briefing.incidentReport: Cars are being admitted incorrectly because the access rules aren't enforced consistently.
briefing.task: Implement decide_entry(vehicle_type, is_vip, has_subscription, garage_full) that returns one of: 'ALLOW', 'DENY', or 'VIP OVERRIDE'.
constraints: VIPs may enter even if the garage is full.
Non-VIPs cannot enter when the garage is full.
Only subscribed drivers may enter when space is available.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #051 — Smart Parking Garage

Implement decide_entry(...) according to the parking rules.
starterCode:
def decide_entry(vehicle_type, is_vip, has_subscription, garage_full):
    pass

hints.text: Break the problem into independent rules before writing code.
Some conditions should be checked before others.
Think about the one situation that bypasses all other rules.

disallow.reason: Return the decision instead of printing it.

onFailFeedback.generic: Review the order of your conditional logic.

title: Incident #063 — Elevator Dispatch System

briefing.context: A building's elevator simulator is behaving unpredictably.
briefing.incidentReport: The elevator should process requests sequentially but immediately stop if an emergency command appears.
briefing.task: Implement process_requests(requests) that returns the floors successfully visited.
constraints: Ignore 'SKIP' requests.
If 'EMERGENCY' appears, stop processing immediately.
Only integer floor numbers should be visited.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #063 — Elevator Dispatch System

Process a queue of elevator requests while handling control commands.

hints.text: Read the requests one at a time in order.
Not every request should be added to the output.
One special command should terminate processing immediately.

disallow.reason: Return the visited floors instead of printing them.

onFailFeedback.generic: Check how you handle SKIP and EMERGENCY requests.

successMessage: Elevator control system is operating correctly.

title: Incident #084 — E-Commerce Pricing Engine

briefing.context: You're maintaining the pricing service for an online store.
briefing.incidentReport: The checkout page repeats the same calculations in multiple places, causing inconsistent totals whenever pricing rules change.
briefing.task: Implement calculate_order_total(subtotal, discount_percent, tax_percent) that returns the final amount after applying the discount first, then tax.
constraints: Do not print the answer; return it.
Round the final result to two decimal places.
Discount is applied before tax.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #084 — E-Commerce Pricing Engine

Implement calculate_order_total(subtotal, discount_percent, tax_percent) for the checkout service.

hints.text: Break the calculation into small intermediate steps.
Apply the discount before calculating tax.
Return the computed value instead of printing it.

disallow.reason: Return the result instead of printing it.

onFailFeedback.generic: Verify the order of operations and ensure you're returning the computed value.

successMessage: Pricing engine now produces consistent checkout totals.

title: Incident #097 — Company Folder Size Analyzer

briefing.context: Your IT department stores folders as nested Python lists.
briefing.incidentReport: Management needs a tool that can count every file inside folders of arbitrary depth.
briefing.task: Implement count_files(folder) that returns the total number of files contained in a nested folder structure.
constraints: Strings represent files.
Lists represent folders.
Folders may be nested to any depth.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #097 — Company Folder Size Analyzer

Implement count_files(folder) to count every file inside a nested folder structure.

hints.text: Consider what the smallest possible folder looks like.
If you encounter another folder, solve the same problem on that folder.
Combine the counts from every nested folder.

disallow.reason: Return the total instead of printing it.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Your solution doesn't appear to call itself recursively.

onFailFeedback.generic: Think about how to handle nested folders of unknown depth.

successMessage: Folder analyzer successfully indexed every file.

title: Incident #112 — Music Streaming Analytics Failure

briefing.context: You're working on the analytics backend for a music streaming platform.
briefing.incidentReport: Duplicate song records and inconsistent counting are producing incorrect daily reports.
briefing.task: Implement analyze_library(songs) that returns a report containing the most played artist, the number of unique songs, and total plays per artist.
constraints: Each song is a dict with keys: title, artist, plays.
Songs with the same title and artist count as one unique song.
Return a dictionary.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #112 — Music Streaming Analytics Failure

Implement analyze_library(songs) to summarize a streaming catalog.

hints.text: Different pieces of information may require different data structures.
One structure is useful for uniqueness while another is useful for totals.
Determine the most played artist after accumulating all play counts.

disallow.reason: Return the report instead of printing it.

onFailFeedback.generic: Check how you're handling duplicates and accumulating play counts.

successMessage: Analytics dashboard is reporting accurate statistics.

title: Incident #128 — Warehouse Inventory Merge

briefing.context: A warehouse has merged inventory from multiple branches.
briefing.incidentReport: Duplicate products appear with different quantities, making stock reports unreliable.
briefing.task: Implement merge_inventory(shipments) that combines all shipments into a single inventory mapping product names to total quantity.
constraints: shipments is a list of dictionaries.
Missing products should be created automatically.
Return one merged dictionary.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #128 — Warehouse Inventory Merge

Implement merge_inventory(shipments) to consolidate warehouse stock.

hints.text: Process one shipment at a time.
Each shipment contains multiple products.
Update a running inventory as you iterate.

disallow.reason: Return the merged inventory instead of printing it.

onFailFeedback.generic: Verify that quantities are accumulated instead of overwritten.

title: Incident #141 — Corrupted Student Grade Archive

briefing.context: You're maintaining the student records system for a school.
briefing.incidentReport: Teachers have submitted a CSV containing every student's grade, but the principal only wants students who scored at least 90 in the honor roll file.
briefing.task: Implement create_honor_roll(input_csv, output_file) that reads the CSV and writes only qualifying student names to the output text file.
constraints: Each CSV row contains Name,Grade.
Create the output file if it doesn't exist.
One student name per line.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #141 — Corrupted Student Grade Archive

Read a CSV of grades and generate an honor roll text file.

hints.text: Open both files safely.
Each row contains a student's name and grade.
Only write students whose grade is at least 90.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Your solution doesn't appear to use context managers or CSV processing.

onFailFeedback.generic: Check that you're correctly reading the CSV and writing only qualifying students.

title: Incident #156 — Customer Backup Recovery

briefing.context: A company's customer backups were split across two JSON files.
briefing.incidentReport: Some customers appear in both backups with updated information. The recovery tool must merge everything into one clean backup.
briefing.task: Implement merge_backups(file_a, file_b, output_file) that combines both JSON files into a single deduplicated backup.
constraints: Customer IDs are unique identifiers.
If a customer exists in both files, the record from file_b replaces file_a.
Write the merged result as JSON.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #156 — Customer Backup Recovery

Merge two JSON backups into one deduplicated backup.

hints.text: Load both JSON files before merging.
Think about which data structure naturally maps IDs to records.
After merging, write the combined data back to disk.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected JSON file processing was not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Verify that duplicate customer IDs are handled correctly and that the merged data is written to the output file.

title: Incident #173 — ATM Transaction Failure

briefing.context: You're maintaining the software for a nationwide ATM network.
briefing.incidentReport: Unexpected inputs are causing ATM terminals to crash instead of displaying helpful messages.
briefing.task: Implement withdraw(balance, amount) that safely processes a withdrawal request.
constraints: Raise ValueError if amount is negative.
Return 'Insufficient funds' if amount exceeds the balance.
Otherwise return the updated balance.
The function should never crash because of invalid numeric input.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #173 — ATM Transaction Failure

Implement withdraw(balance, amount) so invalid transactions are handled safely.

hints.text: Not every input should be treated as valid.
Some situations should return a message while others should raise an exception.
Separate normal execution from exceptional cases.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected exception handling constructs were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Review how exceptional situations differ from normal transactions.

successMessage: ATM network is now resilient against invalid transactions.

title: Incident #189 — Broken Configuration Loader

briefing.context: Your application reads its configuration from disk during startup.
briefing.incidentReport: When the configuration file is missing or malformed, the application crashes instead of recovering gracefully.
briefing.task: Implement load_config(path) that safely loads configuration data.
constraints: If the file is missing, return a default configuration.
If the JSON is invalid, raise ConfigurationError.
Log every failure before handling it.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #189 — Broken Configuration Loader

Implement load_config(path) to safely load application settings.

hints.text: Different failures should be handled differently.
A missing file is recoverable; malformed data is not.
Record failures before returning or raising.

testCases.description: Returns default configuration when file is missing

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected logging or custom exception usage was not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Check that each failure mode is handled according to the incident report.

successMessage: Configuration loader recovered successfully from startup failures.

title: Incident #203 — Hospital Patient Management System

briefing.context: You're developing software for a hospital's patient management system.
briefing.incidentReport: Different patient types are being handled by duplicated code, making updates error-prone.
briefing.task: Design the required classes so doctors can register patients, schedule appointments, and retrieve patient summaries.
constraints: Create a base Patient class.
Support InPatient and OutPatient with their own behavior.
Patient data should not be modified directly from outside the class.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #203 — Hospital Patient Management System

Model a flexible patient management system using object-oriented design.

hints.text: Look for behavior that every patient shares.
Some patient types need specialized behavior.
Think about which attributes should only be changed through methods.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected object-oriented constructs were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Review your class relationships and where responsibilities belong.

title: Incident #224 — Warehouse Robot Fleet

briefing.context: A logistics company controls several kinds of warehouse robots.
briefing.incidentReport: Each robot behaves differently, but the control software currently relies on long chains of if statements.
briefing.task: Design an object model where different robot types share a common interface while implementing their own movement behavior.
constraints: Create a common Robot base class.
Implement at least PickerRobot and CarrierRobot subclasses.
Robot objects should provide meaningful string representations.
Robot status should use an enum.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #224 — Warehouse Robot Fleet

Replace conditional-heavy logic with an extensible object-oriented design.
starterCode:
from enum import Enum

class RobotStatus(Enum):
    IDLE = 1

# Continue your implementation below

hints.text: What behavior is common to every robot?
Subclasses should override behavior rather than duplicating code.
Special methods can make your objects easier to inspect and debug.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected OOP features were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Consider replacing conditionals with polymorphism.

title: Incident #241 — The Monolithic Utility Script

briefing.context: A startup's internal utility has grown into a 1200-line script.
briefing.incidentReport: Every feature is in one file, making maintenance nearly impossible. Engineers accidentally execute code whenever they import the script.
briefing.task: Refactor the project into reusable modules and ensure executable code only runs when appropriate.
constraints: Separate utility functions into logical modules.
The main program should only execute when run directly.
Other modules must be importable without side effects.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #241 — The Monolithic Utility Script

Refactor a large script into a clean, modular Python project.
starterCode:
# project/
# ├── main.py
# ├── math_utils.py
# ├── file_utils.py
# └── string_utils.py

# Complete the project structure.

hints.text: Different responsibilities belong in different modules.
Import reusable code instead of duplicating it.
Prevent accidental execution during imports.

testCases.description: Importing modules causes no side effects.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected module imports or a main guard were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Review your module organization and ensure executable code is protected by a main guard.

successMessage: The project has been successfully modularized.

title: Incident #259 — Broken Developer Environment

briefing.context: A new developer cloned your team's repository.
briefing.incidentReport: The application won't start because dependencies are inconsistent across machines.
briefing.task: Configure the project so another developer can create an isolated environment and install every dependency successfully.
constraints: Use a virtual environment.
Provide dependency information.
Ensure the project metadata is correctly configured.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #259 — Broken Developer Environment

# Files to configure:
# requirements.txt
# pyproject.toml
# README setup instructions

hints.text: Think about what another developer needs before running the project.
Project dependencies should be explicitly listed.
Modern Python projects also include metadata describing the package.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected packaging or dependency-management artifacts were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Ensure another developer can recreate the environment using only your project files.

title: Incident #301 — Infinite Log Stream

briefing.context: Your monitoring system receives millions of server log entries every day.
briefing.incidentReport: The current implementation loads every log into memory before filtering, causing the service to run out of RAM.
briefing.task: Implement filter_errors(log_file) that lazily yields only ERROR log entries matching the expected format.
constraints: Do not load the entire file into memory.
Ignore malformed log lines.
Yield one matching line at a time.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #301 — Infinite Log Stream

Implement filter_errors(log_file) so error logs are processed lazily.

hints.text: Think about producing values one at a time instead of returning a complete list.
A regular expression can validate the log format.
Your solution should scale to files with millions of lines.

testCases.description: Memory usage remains constant for large files.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected generator or regex usage was not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Review how you're processing the file. Avoid building a complete list before returning results.

title: Incident #327 — API Rate Limiter

briefing.context: A public API is receiving too many requests from buggy client applications.
briefing.incidentReport: Developers need a reusable way to prevent a function from being called too frequently.
briefing.task: Implement a decorator rate_limit(max_calls) that blocks additional calls after the configured limit.
constraints: The decorator must preserve independent state for each wrapped function.
Return 'Rate limit exceeded' after the limit is reached.
Include type hints where appropriate.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #327 — API Rate Limiter

Create a reusable decorator that limits how many times a function may be called.

hints.text: The decorator needs somewhere to remember how many calls have occurred.
Inner functions can access variables from their enclosing scope.
Each decorated function should maintain its own independent counter.

testCases.description: Stops execution after the configured number of calls.
Independent decorated functions have separate counters.
Decorator works with different call limits.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected decorator or closure constructs were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Check how state is preserved between function calls.

successMessage: API rate limiter is protecting the service successfully.

title: Incident #351 — Banking Interest Calculation Bug

briefing.context: A bank deployed a new interest calculation service.
briefing.incidentReport: Customers report incorrect balances, but nobody knows which inputs trigger the bug because there are almost no automated tests.
briefing.task: Write a comprehensive test suite for calculate_interest(), identify the failing behavior, then fix the implementation without breaking existing functionality.
constraints: Write tests before modifying the implementation.
Cover normal cases, edge cases, and invalid inputs.
Keep the public API unchanged.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #351 — Banking Interest Calculation Bug

Use automated tests to locate and fix a defect in a financial calculation library.
starterCode:
# calculate_interest() has already been implemented.
# Your job is to write tests, discover the bug, and fix it.

hints.text: Start by describing the expected behavior with tests.
Boundary values often expose hidden defects.
Only change the implementation after a failing test demonstrates the bug.

testCases.description: Regression tests catch the original bug.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected automated tests were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Focus on writing tests that describe the expected behavior before modifying the implementation.

successMessage: Banking bug eliminated and protected by automated tests.

title: Incident #372 — Unstable Weather Service

briefing.context: Your application retrieves weather data from an external API.
briefing.incidentReport: Tests randomly fail because they depend on the live internet service.
briefing.task: Write reliable tests by mocking the external API so the application can be tested offline.
constraints: Do not contact the real API during testing.
Mock successful and failed responses.
Refactor only if necessary to improve testability.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #372 — Unstable Weather Service

Replace unreliable network-dependent tests with deterministic mocked tests.
starterCode:
# fetch_weather(city) is already implemented.
# Write a reliable test suite using mocking.

hints.text: Replace the external dependency rather than calling it.
Test both successful and failed requests.
Your tests should pass without an internet connection.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected mocking constructs were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Your tests should isolate the code from external services by using mocks.

successMessage: Weather service test suite is now fast and deterministic.

title: Incident #401 — Food Delivery Dispatch Meltdown

briefing.context: You're building the backend for a food delivery platform.
briefing.incidentReport: Orders are currently prepared one at a time, causing massive delays during dinner hours.
briefing.task: Implement dispatch_orders(orders) so multiple restaurant orders can be prepared concurrently while collecting every completed order.
constraints: Each order is independent.
The returned list should contain every completed order.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #401 — Food Delivery Dispatch Meltdown

Process restaurant orders concurrently to reduce customer wait times.

hints.text: Each order can be processed independently.

Collect results safely after every worker finishes.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected concurrency constructs were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Ensure orders are processed concurrently while preserving all results.

successMessage: Food delivery system now handles peak-hour demand efficiently.

title: Incident #428 — Satellite Image Processing Queue

briefing.context: A space agency receives thousands of satellite images every hour.
briefing.incidentReport: CPU-intensive image analysis is blocking new downloads, causing the processing queue to grow indefinitely.
briefing.task: Build a pipeline that downloads image metadata asynchronously and analyzes image data in parallel.
constraints: Network operations should not block each other.
CPU-intensive work should run independently of downloads.
Return processed image summaries.

descriptionHtml:

Design a concurrent pipeline that combines asynchronous downloads with parallel computation.
starterCode:
async def download_metadata(image_ids):
    pass

# Implement the remaining pipeline

hints.text: Downloading metadata and analyzing images have different performance characteristics.
One task spends most of its time waiting, while the other uses the CPU.
Combine asynchronous programming with parallel execution.

testCases.description: Pipeline scales efficiently with many images.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected async or multiprocessing constructs were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Separate I/O-bound work from CPU-bound work and choose appropriate concurrency tools.

successMessage: Satellite processing pipeline restored to real-time performance.

title: Incident #452 — Search Engine Slowdown

briefing.context: You're working on the backend of a search engine.

Repeated linear searches often become expensive.
Consider whether another data structure could reduce lookup time.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected profiling or optimization techniques were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Optimize only after identifying the performance bottleneck. Preserve the original behavior.

title: Incident #479 — Social Media Feed Memory Leak

briefing.context: A social media platform keeps crashing after several hours of continuous use.

title: Incident #503 — Downloads Folder Disaster

briefing.context: IT receives complaints that employees can't find downloaded files.

title: Incident #528 — Automated Deployment Pipeline

briefing.context: Your company deploys applications manually using terminal commands.

title: Incident #701 — Disease Outbreak Analytics

briefing.context: A public health agency receives daily disease reports from hundreds of hospitals.
briefing.incidentReport: Analysts are manually processing spreadsheets, delaying critical decisions during an outbreak.
briefing.task: Build a data analysis pipeline that loads the dataset, computes key statistics, identifies unusual trends, and generates summary visualizations.
constraints: Use Pandas for data processing.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #701 — Disease Outbreak Analytics

Analyze a real-world dataset and generate actionable insights for public health officials.
starterCode:
# outbreak.csv is provided.
# Analyze the data and generate a report.

hints.text: Load the dataset into a DataFrame before analyzing it.
Aggregate statistics often reveal hidden trends.
Visualizations should communicate the important findings clearly.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected data analysis libraries were not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Verify your analysis pipeline and ensure the reported statistics match the dataset.

title: Incident #728 — Release Engineering Crisis

briefing.context: Your team's internal analytics tool is being released to customers for the first time.
briefing.incidentReport: Users cannot install or run the application because the package has not been prepared correctly.
briefing.task: Package the application into an installable Python project with a command-line entry point and verify it can be installed into a clean environment.
constraints: Configure package metadata.
Expose a CLI entry point.
Ensure installation works in a fresh virtual environment.
Document installation steps.

descriptionHtml:
Incident #728 — Release Engineering Crisis

Prepare a Python application for distribution to end users.
starterCode:
# Project skeleton provided.
# Configure packaging and distribution files.

hints.text: A project intended for users needs more than working source code.
Think about how users install and launch the application.
Package metadata and entry points are both required for a polished release.

testCases.description: Metadata and distribution files are valid.

requireAny.reasonIfMissing: Expected packaging configuration was not detected.

onFailFeedback.generic: Ensure the project can be installed and executed on a clean machine.
